scheme.name: Interest Subvention Support under Export Promotion Mission (Niryat Protsahan)

provider.name: DGFT (Ministry of Commerce and Industry); RBI reimbursement to banks

benefits.name: Interest subvention on pre and post shipment rupee export credit (MSME manufacturers)
benefits.summary: Reduction in the interest rate on pre- and post-shipment rupee export credit for eligible MSME manufacturer exporters, continuing the erstwhile Interest Equalisation Scheme under the new Export Promotion Mission framework.
benefits.calculation_logic: Interest subvention of about 2.75pct (historically 3pct) per annum for MSME manufacturer exporters on rupee export credit, subject to an annual per-MSME ceiling (a 50 lakh cap applied in FY 2024-25). Current rate and cap governed by DGFT trade notices under the Export Promotion Mission (Jan 2026).

metadata.notes: Successor to the Interest Equalisation Scheme (IES), now under the Export Promotion Mission (Niryat Protsahan, outlay 25,060 crore FY26-31). Requires a DGFT-generated UIN submitted to the bank; goods must originate in India. Confirm current rate and annual cap from the latest DGFT trade notice.

suggested_faqs.question: What is the main objective of Interest Subvention Support under Export Promotion Mission (Niryat Protsahan)?
suggested_faqs.answer: The main objective is to provide assistance and support under Interest Subvention Support under Export Promotion Mission (Niryat Protsahan) to eligible beneficiaries.

suggested_faqs.question: Who is eligible to apply for Interest Subvention Support under Export Promotion Mission (Niryat Protsahan)?
suggested_faqs.answer: Businesses belonging to micro, small, medium, exporter in manufacturing, export_oriented may apply.
